# UC-22.61.5 · DSL Art. 21 Important Data Catalogue freshness, classification coverage, and sectoral Important Data List alignment tracker

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Governance &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*China divides company data into important, very-important, and regular tiers. Each year the government can update which data is in which tier. We make sure every dataset has the right tier label and the labels are fresh.*

---

## Description

Reconciles every dataset across the organisation against the DSL Art. 21 four-tier classification (General / Important / Core / other-sectoral) and against the sectoral protection department's Important Data List. Flags datasets that lack classification, datasets whose classification is stale (>12 months), and datasets classified as Important or Core that lack a sectoral-list cross-reference. Surfaces missing or stale sectoral lists as a sibling alert so that the operator's catalogue stays in lock-step with the regulator-published list.

## Value

DSL Art. 52 sets the fine ceiling for failure to perform DSL data-protection duties at RMB 200,000 - 2,000,000 per violation, plus RMB 20,000 - 200,000 on the responsible individual, plus suspension of business and revocation of licence. Misclassifying Important Data as General Data is also material to PIPL Art. 38 cross-border decisions (Important Data triggers mandatory CAC security assessment, while General Data does not); a misclassification therefore cascades into UC-22.61.4 enforcement gaps and into PIPL fines under Art. 66 (RMB 50 million / 5% turnover). For Core Data, the consequences include criminal liability under PRC Criminal Law for unauthorised disclosure.

## Implementation

(1) Populate `cn_important_data_catalog_lookup` from the existing data-catalogue tool (Microsoft Purview, Collibra, Alation, BigID, OneTrust) extended with the four DSL tiers. (2) Maintain `cn_sectoral_important_data_list` against the current sectoral Important Data List publication. Reload every 90 days or on regulator update. (3) The DPO + sectoral compliance lead reviews each Important / Core classification quarterly. (4) Schedule UC-22.61.5 daily at 06:00 Beijing time; route `risk_score >= 70` to the DPO + Business Unit Owner at severity P2.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.61.5` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `cn_csl_clause="DSL-Art-21"` and `data_tier`. Every row pins (dataset_id, dataset_name, business_unit, data_tier, sectoral_list_alignment, last_classification_date, days_since_classification, owner, risk_score).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Onboard a new dataset in a CIIO business unit without performing classification within 30 days of go-live. UC-22.61.5 MUST flag with risk_score >= 70.

### Negative scenario

Classify a new dataset within 7 days of onboarding, align to the current sectoral Important Data List, and update the catalogue. UC-22.61.5 MUST record risk_score=0.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise >= 9.2 or Splunk Cloud (PRC region for in-PRC datasets).
- A data-catalogue platform with API export (Microsoft Purview, Collibra, Alation, BigID, OneTrust).
- KV Store `cn_important_data_catalog_lookup` seeded daily from the data-catalogue API.
- KV Store `cn_sectoral_important_data_list` curated quarterly from sectoral publications.

## Step 1 - Catalogue KV Stores

```ini
[cn_important_data_catalog_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.dataset_id = string
field.dataset_name = string
field.business_unit = string
field.hosting_region = string
field.data_tier = string
field.sectoral_list_ref = string
field.last_classification_date = time
field.classifier_id = string
field.owner = string
field.project_status = string

[cn_sectoral_important_data_list]
enforceTypes = true
field.sectoral_list_ref = string
field.sectoral_department = string
field.list_version = string
field.effective_date = time
field.list_categories = string
```

## Step 2 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.61.5: DSL Important Data Catalogue Freshness]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.61.5,reg=CN-CSL,clause=DSL-Art-21,tier=$data_tier$
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 3 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 80`** (owner: DPO, severity P2): Within 5 business days, complete classification of the dataset and align to the sectoral list.

**`risk_score 40-79`** (owner: Business Unit Data Steward, severity P3): Within 10 business days, refresh classification and confirm sectoral-list alignment.

## Step 4 - Sectoral-specific notes

- **Automotive**: 2021 Provisions on Automotive Data Security Management list six categories of Important Data — military / state-organ surveys, geographic data including >10cm-precision images, road-vehicle flows including continuous-tracking, intersection / parking-lot CCTV >100 cameras, etc.
- **Industrial / MIIT**: 2020 Industrial Data Classification and Grading Guide defines five-grade severity classification specific to industrial sectors.
- **Telecom / MIIT**: industrial-internet sector list updates 2022-2024; track quarterly.
- **Finance / CBIRC**: banking sector data-classification guidelines (JR/T 0197-2020).

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup cn_important_data_catalog_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_since_classification = if(isnotnull(last_classification_date), round((now_ts - last_classification_date) / 86400, 1), null())
| lookup cn_sectoral_important_data_list sectoral_list_ref OUTPUT list_version effective_date
| eval list_stale = if(isnotnull(effective_date) AND (now_ts - effective_date) > (365 * 86400), 1, 0)
| eval classification_missing = if(isnull(last_classification_date) OR isnull(data_tier), 1, 0)
| eval classification_stale = if(isnotnull(days_since_classification) AND days_since_classification > 365, 1, 0)
| eval important_or_core = if(data_tier IN ("Important Data","Core Data"), 1, 0)
| eval sectoral_alignment_missing = if(important_or_core = 1 AND isnull(sectoral_list_ref), 1, 0)
| eval risk_score = case(
    classification_missing = 1, 80,
    sectoral_alignment_missing = 1 AND data_tier = "Core Data", 95,
    sectoral_alignment_missing = 1, 75,
    classification_stale = 1 AND important_or_core = 1, 70,
    classification_stale = 1, 40,
    list_stale = 1 AND important_or_core = 1, 50,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, +days_since_classification
| table dataset_id dataset_name business_unit data_tier sectoral_list_ref last_classification_date days_since_classification classification_missing classification_stale sectoral_alignment_missing list_stale risk_score
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Datasets by tier (General / Important / Core / Sectoral). (Panel 2) Classification freshness heat map. (Panel 3) Sectoral-list alignment gaps. (Panel 4) Important Data Catalogue trend (12 months).

## Known False Positives

1. **New dataset in onboarding**: a dataset that was provisioned within the last 30 days may be in its initial classification window; mark with `project_status=onboarding` and exempt during the 30-day window. 2. **Pending reclassification**: a dataset under quarterly re-review may briefly be in `under-review` status; load the review_started_at and treat as in-window for up to 30 days. 3. **Sectoral list silent update**: a sectoral department may issue a minor refresh of the Important Data List without changing the version number; refresh the catalogue every 90 days unconditionally and compare diffs.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `cn_csl_22_61_5_exceptions` with the DPO documented approval, an explicit expiry date, and a written justification.

## References

- [Data Security Law of the People's Republic of China (2021) - Article 21](http://www.npc.gov.cn/npc/c2/c30834/202106/t20210610_311888.html)
- [Provisions on Automotive Data Security Management (2021)](https://www.cac.gov.cn/)
- [Industrial Data Classification and Grading Guide (2020, MIIT)](https://www.miit.gov.cn/)
